purpose: Authoritative, technical orientation for coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, etc.). Prefer reading named source files over trusting stale prose; use this file to route quickly to the right module, API, env var, or UI route.

when_to_update: Bump `repo.app_version` when `server.py` VERSION changes; mirror `index.html` cache-bust `?v=` and footer label per About page note.
After adding/removing HTTP routes in `server.py`, sync `http_api.routes`.
After DB schema changes in `backend/db.py`, sync `persistence.sqlite`.
After new hash routes or API consumers in `js/app.js`, sync `frontend`.
maintenance.anchor_convention: Paths are repo-relative. Python symbols use `module.qualname`. JSON keys in API bodies are literal strings.

repo.one_liner: Local-only web app: read-only scan of per-customer Helm custom_values YAML trees, deep-merged with a base chart values.yaml, persisted in SQLite, browsed via vanilla HTML/JS/CSS with optional Gemini NL filters and optional Prometheus-backed version matrices.

runtime.http_stack: stdlib `http.server.ThreadingHTTPServer` + `SimpleHTTPRequestHandler` subclass; static files served from repo root (`ROOT`).
runtime.launcher: run.sh (creates .venv, pip install -r requirements.txt, exec python server.py)
runtime.pythonpath_note: `server.py` inserts repo root into `sys.path` then imports `backend.*`.

tree_roles.server.py: HTTP server, routing table for `/api/*`, imports payload builders from `backend.api_payloads`, orchestrates scan and Gemini/Prometheus helpers.
tree_roles.backend/config.py: Paths, defaults, env resolution, Gemini key resolution, `DATA_DIR`, `DB_PATH`, `USER_CONFIG_FILE`, `CORE_SEGMENT_SERVICE_KEY` (default service key for Core segment).
tree_roles.backend/db.py: SQLite schema, `connect` context manager, migrations (`_migrate`), CRUD for `customers` + `meta`.
tree_roles.backend/scan.py: Filesystem walk, YAML load, `deep_merge` chain, `discover_service_keys`/`get_service_block`/`effective_enabled` for core flag, `run_scan_to_db`.
tree_roles.backend/api_payloads.py: All structured JSON/HTML CSV payloads for UI: `meta_bundle`, `dashboard_matrix`, `service_dive`, diffs, anomaly, services list, CSV.
tree_roles.backend/prometheus_versions.py: `fetch_version_points`, `fetch_k8s_version_points`, `fetch_heatmap_cells`, `fetch_heatmap_bad_pod_rows`, `heatmap_bad_pod_detail_row`, `heatmap_status`, `_instant_query_vector`; PromQL includes heatmap aggregate + per-customer bad-pod detail query builder `_heatmap_bad_pods_detail_query`.
tree_roles.index.html: Shell + nav links; cache-bust `css/app.css?v=` and `js/app.js?v=` must track `server.py` VERSION.
tree_roles.js/app.js: Hash router, fetches `/api/*`, matrix/dive/anomaly/Prometheus UI, tooltip pin, Gemini UI wiring.
tree_roles.data/: Writable runtime dir (gitignored patterns); holds `analyzer.db`, `user_config.json`, `server.pid`, optional `.gemini_api_key`.

configuration.path_resolution_priority: For customers root and base values: environment variable overrides `data/user_config.json` overrides hardcoded defaults in `backend/config.py`.

environment_variables.name: CVA_CUSTOMERS_ROOT
environment_variables.meaning: Filesystem root containing customer trees (expected `AWS/` and `Azure/` subdirs, or single-cloud subdir, or flat legacy layout).

environment_variables.name: CVA_BASE_VALUES
environment_variables.meaning: Path to base Helm `values.yaml` merged under every customer overlay.

environment_variables.name: CVA_CORE_SERVICE
environment_variables.meaning: Top-level service key whose `enabled: true` marks a customer as Core for segmentation (`core_segment` column, `customer_core` maps).

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_API_KEY
environment_variables.meaning: Primary API key for Gemini NL filter (Service Dive).

environment_variables.name: GOOGLE_API_KEY
environment_variables.meaning: Alternate env name for same key.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_API_KEY_FILE
environment_variables.meaning: Path to text file; first non-empty line is the key.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_MODEL
environment_variables.meaning: If set, only this sanitized model id is used for NL filter.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_MODEL_FALLBACKS
environment_variables.meaning: Comma-separated model ids tried in order when `GEMINI_MODEL` unset.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_TIMEOUT_SEC
environment_variables.meaning: HTTP timeout seconds per Gemini request.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_NL_MAX_MATRIX_KEYS
environment_variables.meaning: Cap rows from Service Dive matrix included in NL context (clamped 20..300).

environment_variables.name: CVA_PROMETHEUS_URL
environment_variables.meaning: Prometheus instant query API base (GET with `query=`).

keys: gemini_key_file (optional; legacy openai_key_file removed on save)

persistent_files.meaning: SQLite DB of merged customer documents.
Current server PID for operator UX.
Saved API key from UI (chmod 600 on POSIX); also scanned by resolve_gemini_api_key.

defaults_in_code.cloud_subdirs_canonical: ('AWS', 'Azure') — see backend.config.CLOUD_SUBDIRS and scan._canonical_cloud_dirs case-insensitive matching.

sqlite.connection: backend.db.connect(db_path) -> sqlite3.Connection with row_factory Row; runs SCHEMA + _migrate on entry.

columns: name TEXT UNIQUE — customer key e.g. AWS/foo or legacy flat name
source_files TEXT NOT NULL — JSON list of {name, path}
merged_json TEXT NOT NULL — full merged values dict as JSON
core_segment INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 — 1 if CORE_SEGMENT_SERVICE_KEY enabled true at scan

layouts: Parent with AWS/ and Azure/ subdirs → names `Cloud/CustomerDir`
Root named aws or azure (case-insensitive) → enumerate child dirs under that cloud
Else flat: each non-hidden child dir is a customer name without cloud prefix

scanning.per_customer_merge_order: Start dict copy of base (`_load_base`), then for each overlay file in sorted order apply `backend.merge.deep_merge`.
scanning.core_flag_at_scan: backend.scan.scan_customers uses `get_service_block(merged, CORE_SEGMENT_SERVICE_KEY)` and `effective_enabled(blk) is True` → core_segment 1 else 0.

merge.semantics: If both sides are dicts, recursively merge keys; child dicts merge recursively; non-dict or new keys replace with deepcopy of override. If base is not a mapping, returns deepcopy(override).

service_model.discover_service_keys: backend.services.discover_service_keys(merged) -> sorted list of logical service ids.
rules: Skip root keys in backend.services.SKIP_ROOT: global, glassbox, tags, dumpScript, dependencies, imageAdmissionPolicy, glassboxConfig.
If `cronjobs` is dict: include `cronjobs.<child>` when child dict has `enabled` key.
If `siteGeneric` is dict: include `siteGeneric.<child>` when child dict has `enabled` key.
Other top-level dict children with `enabled` key are services (component-style chart values).
service_model.get_service_block: backend.services.get_service_block(merged, service_key) resolves cronjobs.* and siteGeneric.* paths; else merged[service_key] if dict else {}.
service_model.effective_enabled: backend.services.effective_enabled(block) reads block['enabled'] with truthy coercion for bool/int/str.
service_model.matrix_cell_semantics: In `dashboard_matrix`, cell `enabled` is true iff `effective_enabled(block) is True`; red/green UI treats any non-true as red.

yaml_serialization.dump_yaml: PyYAML yaml.dump default_flow_style=False, sort_keys=False, width=100; optional max_len truncation with trailing comment line.

conventions: JSON responses use Handler._json; static assets get Cache-Control no-cache headers in Handler.end_headers.
Many GET endpoints return empty structures if `DB_PATH` missing — UI treats as no data.

routes.response: { customers_root, base_values } strings
{ ok: true, customers_root, base_values }
version, name, description, features[], tech, source_paths — mirrors app marketing copy
{ has_data, pid, meta } where meta from backend.api_payloads.meta_bundle
Array of { name, file_count, files[], scan_epoch, core }
backend.api_payloads.customer_public_view — includes merged_yaml string
backend.api_payloads.services_list_payload — { core_service_key, customer_core, services: [{ name, in_core, in_other, in_aws, in_azure }] }
backend.api_payloads.service_public_view — { name, by_customer: { cust: { enabled, yaml } } }
backend.api_payloads.dashboard_matrix
backend.api_payloads.compute_anomaly — [{ name, count, total }]
backend.api_payloads.service_dive or 404
backend.api_payloads.service_yaml_diff_payload — { service, left, right, mode, html } or { error }
backend.api_payloads.customer_yaml_diff_payload
text/csv from backend.api_payloads.matrix_csv
server.py._gemini_status_payload — configured, source, saved_key_file_exists, user_key_file_configured

routes.effect: backend.config save/clear key file paths and optional disk key

routes.flow: connect DB → service_dive → build_service_dive_nl_context → call_gemini_nl_filter → { ok, filter, model }

routes.response: { points: [{ customer, version, count }] } or { error } with 502/504
{ points: [{ customer, cluster, k8s_version, region, count }] }
{ customer, pods: [{ customer, cluster, region, namespace, pod, container, status, reason }] } or { error }
routes.python: server.py.Handler.do_GET → backend.prometheus_versions.fetch_heatmap_bad_pod_rows (single instant query; lazy UI on tile click)

routes.response: { customers: [{ customer, node_count, error_pods, status: GREEN|ORANGE|RED }] } or { error }
{ ok, customers_scanned, service_keys, errors, customers_root, base_values_path }

routes.effect: Thread sleeps 0.4s, deletes PID file, ThreadingHTTPServer.shutdown

shape.row_keys: string[] union of 2nd-level keys across per-customer service blocks

matrix.modal_pct: float percentage of customers sharing modal serialized value

<customer>.yaml: hover text: dump_yaml for dict/list else scalar line
<customer>.is_modal: bool (legacy name; UI recomputes modal client-side in js renderDiveTable)
<customer>.is_missing: bool when key absent for customer

gemini_nl_filter.ui_application: js/app.js Service Dive AI branch sets dive-fkey, dive-fcust, first kv row, cloud/core radios from filter; client still applies kv logic in filterCustomersByKVList.

prometheus_integration.heatmap: HEATMAP_NODES_BY_CUSTOMER_QUERY + HEATMAP_ERROR_PODS_QUERY; fetch_heatmap_cells merges by Customer; heatmap_status(0→GREEN, 1–2→ORANGE, 3+→RED); fetch_heatmap_bad_pod_rows + heatmap_bad_pod_detail_row for GET /api/heatmap/bad-pods (per-customer instant vector, same kube selectors as aggregate)

frontend.shell: index.html — #app main mount, #tooltip floating yaml, #nav routes, footer #footer-status

hash_routes.notes: D3 treemap ESM from jsDelivr; bad-pod table only after tile click

notable_client_logic: Matrix CSV download: client-generated filtered matrix (matrix_filtered.csv), not only server /api/export/matrix.csv
Service Dive: filterCustomersByKV / filterCustomersByKVList AND semantics; renderDiveTable recomputes modal/outlier coloring per visible customer subset
Prometheus matrices: matchCustomerRowFromScan maps Prom Customer label to scanned customer rows for cloud/core filters

modes.diff: HtmlDiff.make_table context=True numlines=0 — changed lines only
modes.all: context=False numlines=5 — full YAML with diff highlights

testing.focus: normalize_nl_filter, build_service_dive_nl_context, call_gemini_nl_filter with mocked urllib

security_and_ops_notes: App designed for local use; binds loopback only in server.py.main.
Gemini keys resolved from env, files, or UI persistence under data/ — see backend.config.resolve_gemini_api_key order.
Scan is read-only on customer YAML; writes limited to data/ SQLite and config.
Prometheus default URL points at an org endpoint; override with CVA_PROMETHEUS_URL for other environments.
